description: This command initializes pore pressures, changing the effective stresses. FLAC uses effective stresses (i.e., total stresses plus pore pressure) in constitutive models. Pore pressures are defined at gridpoints, and zone pore pressures are then calculated as the average of the zone gridpoints. If model configure fluid is not set, pore pressures are not affected by zone volume changes, there is no flow of water, and the dry density must be specified for zones above the water table and the saturated material density for zones below.

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: fluid density, ρw [SI units: kg/m3]. This will force a recalculation of pore pressures on all gridpoints in the range.

keywords.name: density-effective
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the total stresses in all zones connected to gridpoints in the range will be adjusted to keep the effective stress constant.

keywords.name: density-skip-assignment
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the values used to calculate pore-pressures is changed to pore-pressures are not actually assigned to any gridpoints. The values specified will remain until the next zone water command is given.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: Lists the current water table settings. This includes the assumed fluid density, the geometric set to use (if any), and the water table plane last specified.

keywords.name: set
keywords.description: Specify the geometric set used to determine water surface location. By default, the name is Water Surface. Specifying a new set name causes a recalculation of gridpoint pore pressures for all gridpoints below polygons in that set that are also in the range. See the Geometry section.

keywords.name: set-effective
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the total stresses in all zones connected to gridpoints in the range will be adjusted to keep the effective stress constant.

keywords.name: set-skip-assignment
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the values used to calculate pore-pressures is changed to pore-pressures are not actually assigned to any gridpoints. The values specified will remain until the next zone water command is given.

keywords.name: plane
keywords.description: Specify a water table plane. The water table sets pore pressure for all gridpoints (and, hence, zones) below the water table. The pore-pressure gradient is given by the direction of the gravity vector, which can be arbitrary (see the model gravity command).

keywords.name: plane-effective
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the total stresses in all zones connected to gridpoints in the range will be adjusted to keep the effective stress constant.

keywords.name: plane-normal
keywords.description: normal direction to the plane, defined by unit vector v and pointing in the direction of increasing pore pressure.

keywords.name: plane-origin
keywords.description: one point at coordinate location (v) on the plane

keywords.name: plane-skip-assignment
keywords.description: the plane to use for the purpose of calculate pore pressures is specified, but pore pressures are not actually assigned to gridpoints in the range.

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: fluid density, \(ρ_w\) [SI units: kg/m3]. This will force a recalculation of pore pressures on all gridpoints in the range.

keywords.name: density-effective
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the total stresses in all zones connected to gridpoints in the range will be adjusted to keep the effective stress constant.

keywords.name: density-skip-assignment
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the values used to calculate pore-pressures is changed to pore-pressures are not actually assigned to any gridpoints. The values specified will remain until the next zone water command is given.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: Lists the current water table settings. This includes the assumed fluid density, the geometric set to use (if any), and the water table plane last specified.

keywords.name: set
keywords.description: Specify the geometric set used to determine water surface location. By default, the name is Water Surface. Specifying a new set name causes a recalculation of gridpoint pore pressures for all gridpoints below polygons in that set that are also in the range. See the geometry command.

keywords.name: set-effective
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the total stresses in all zones connected to gridpoints in the range will be adjusted to keep the effective stress constant.

keywords.name: set-skip-assignment
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the values used to calculate pore-pressures is changed to pore-pressures are not actually assigned to any gridpoints. The values specified will remain until the next zone water command is given.

keywords.name: plane
keywords.description: Specify a water table plane. The water table sets pore pressure for all gridpoints (and, hence, zones) below the water table. The pore-pressure gradient is given by the direction of the gravity vector, which can be arbitrary (see the model gravity command).

keywords.name: plane-effective
keywords.description: If this keyword is specified, then the total stresses in all zones connected to gridpoints in the range will be adjusted to keep the effective stress constant.

keywords.name: plane-normal
keywords.description: normal direction to the plane, defined by unit vector v and pointing in the direction of increasing pore pressure.

keywords.name: plane-origin
keywords.description: one point at coordinate location (v) on the plane

keywords.name: plane-skip-assignment
keywords.description: the plane to use for the purpose of calculate pore pressures is specified, but pore pressures are not actually assigned to gridpoints in the range.

keywords.name: clear
keywords.description: Clear all water-table settings (density, set, plane, table) and reset the module to defaults.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List the current water-table settings (density, water-surface mode, table assignments).

keywords.name: table
keywords.description: Use a named table to define the water surface elevation as a function of position. (Stub for the 9.0 table-driven water surface API.)

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: Assign water density f to zones in the range. Sub-keywords: effective, skip-assignment.

keywords.name: density-effective
keywords.description: Apply the density assignment using the effective-stress formulation (subtract pore-pressure-equivalent body force).

keywords.name: density-skip-assignment
keywords.description: Skip applying the density change to gridpoint body-force contributions; useful when only the constitutive-model density is intended to change.

keywords.name: set
keywords.description: Set the water level via named gridpoint set s. Sub-keywords: effective, skip-assignment.

keywords.name: set-effective
keywords.description: Apply the set using the effective-stress formulation.

keywords.name: set-skip-assignment
keywords.description: Skip applying the resulting density/body-force change to gridpoints; only update the recorded set.

keywords.name: plane
keywords.description: Define the water surface as a plane. Sub-keywords: effective, normal v, origin v, skip-assignment.

keywords.name: plane-effective
keywords.description: Apply the planar water surface using the effective-stress formulation.

keywords.name: plane-normal
keywords.description: Normal vector of the water-surface plane.

keywords.name: plane-origin
keywords.description: Origin point of the water-surface plane (a point that lies on the plane).

keywords.name: plane-skip-assignment
keywords.description: Skip applying the resulting body-force change to gridpoints; only record the plane.
